summary: First published edition. Added verified guidance on checkpoint recovery, compaction survival, subagent scoping, turn-gating hooks, fresh-context review, and permission enforcement. One plausible cache claim was excluded for failing verification.

changes.item: Recover instead of fighting a bad path: /rewind (or double-Esc on an empty prompt) rolls back code and/or conversation; /btw asks throwaway questions that never enter history.
changes.rationale: Both commands and the Esc-Esc shortcut are confirmed verbatim in the official interactive-mode and checkpointing docs.

changes.item: Project-root CLAUDE.md re-injects after /compact; nested CLAUDE.md files and chat-only instructions do not — keep must-survive rules in the root file.
changes.rationale: Stated explicitly in the official memory documentation.

changes.item: Subagents start blind to your session — scope them narrowly; the built-in Explore and Plan agents skip CLAUDE.md, so restate any rule they must follow in the delegation prompt.
changes.rationale: Confirmed in the official sub-agents documentation.

changes.item: A Stop hook can block a turn from ending until your check script succeeds — deterministic where CLAUDE.md is only advisory.
changes.rationale: Confirmed in the official hooks documentation.

changes.item: Before declaring done, have a fresh subagent review the diff against the requirements; tell it to flag only gaps affecting correctness or stated requirements, or it will invent work.
changes.rationale: Reflects the review-subagent guidance in the official best-practices documentation.

changes.item: Permissions are enforced by Claude Code, not the model: a deny rule or PreToolUse hook blocks an action regardless of what CLAUDE.md or the prompt says.
changes.rationale: Confirmed in the official permissions documentation.

changes.item: Block-level HTML comments are stripped before CLAUDE.md loads — free for maintainer notes, unlike prose and '#' lines which consume tokens.
changes.rationale: Confirmed in the official memory documentation.

changes.item: Plan mode is worth it for multi-file or uncertain-scope work, but skip it when you could describe the diff in one sentence.
changes.rationale: Reflects the planning guidance in the official best-practices documentation.

changes.item: Line-budget guidance now carries a concrete test: for each line ask 'would removing this cause a mistake?' If not, cut it. Target aligned to under 200 lines.
changes.rationale: Matches the official memory docs (under 200 lines; bloated files reduce adherence), corroborated by community sources.

excluded_detail.item: Put stable rules at the top of CLAUDE.md and volatile notes at the bottom to maximise prompt-cache hits.
excluded_detail.reason: Not stated in the official docs — prompt-cache hit-rate ordering is community inference, not documented Claude Code behaviour. Failed the accuracy gate.
